channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Trump's annual financial disclosure shows more than $580M in crypto-related income
item.description: Trump's 927-page financial disclosure report for 2025 totals more than 900 pages detailing holdings in cryptocurrency and stocks.

item.title: Iran says it is selling oil at 20% premium as end of U.S. blockade sees 40 million barrels exported
item.description: The ceasefire prompted a surge in crude shipments through the Strait of Hormuz, where traffic had largely ground to a halt during the conflict.

item.title: LNG market disruption may continue for months as a top producer withholds some Italian shipments
item.description: QatarEnergy extended its force majeure, withholding four additional liquefied natural gas cargoes until September.

item.title: Yen slides to new 40-year low while Dow futures ease after strongest first half in five years: Live updates
item.description: The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 8.9% in the first half of 2026.

item.title: Anthropic says Trump admin has lifted export controls on Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5
item.description: The U.S. Department of Commerce lifted the export controls on Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models

item.title: Employers who laid off workers citing AI are already starting to regret it
item.description: Companies are realizing artificial intelligence can't do everything after all, prompting them to rehire employees to grow their businesses

item.title: Nike results top estimates even as China sales drop 12%; retailer expects $986 million tariff refund
item.description: Nike was expected to report another quarter of declining sales as the sneaker giant struggles to regain strength under its turnaround strategy.

item.title: U.S. military deploys over 900 personnel to Venezuela for earthquake response
item.description: U.S. ‌forces joined search-and-rescue operations, restored airport operations and mobilized air and naval assets to speed humanitarian aid after the earthquakes.

item.title: CNBC Daily Open: AI demand fuels investors' portfolios while oil posts biggest monthly decline
item.description: The tech space continues to be fueled by rising artificial intelligence demand, with investors piling their portfolios in the technology, while Amazon Web Services dabbles with Forward Deployed Engineering units to better compete with OpenAI and Anthropic.

item.title: Where to find top CD yields in the second half as Fed policy remains murky
item.description: Banks are fine-tuning their approach toward deposits, with some trimming rates on high-yield savings accounts while others lift rates on CDs.

item.title: Asia’s IPO boom looks strong. Here’s why it may be more complicated
item.description: Asia-Pacific is leading the global IPO market, but its boom is not one story. Hong Kong, Mainland China and India are each powered by a different IPO engine — offshore liquidity, policy-guided capital and domestic retail demand. This CNBC Explains looks at why raising billions is only the first test, and why the harder question is whether that demand lasts after the opening bell.

item.title: Trump's massive defense budget, depleted war machine, spark U.S. state battle for business and jobs
item.description: Trump's huge defense budget request and a race to replenish weapons stocks while building hypersonic missiles lead to war between U.S. states for jobs.

item.title: Jim Cramer says the AI trade has shifted — and these stocks are leading now
item.description: CNBC's Jim Cramer said Wall Street is rewarding the companies supplying the artificial intelligence boom rather than the technology giants funding it.

item.title: Record chip rally adds $2 trillion in combined value to Micron, Intel and AMD in second quarter
item.description: Wall Street poured into chipmakers not named Nvidia in the second quarter, as the artificial intelligence boom expanded to include more suppliers.

item.title: Where you want to invest in tech for the second half, according to one top analyst
item.description: Frontier models need access to increasing volumes of maneuverable data. Software companies that can supply it will be crucial in the entire ecosystem.

item.title: Palo Alto, CrowdStrike both have best quarter ever as AI threats bolster cyber demand
item.description: The cybersecurity leaders are leaning into identity security, a critical cyber segment, as AI agents outnumber humans

item.title: Do you really, really love your job? Then you're not alone, according to surprising results from this survey
item.description: Those surveyed showed a 78.9% rate of workers who "reported feeling positive at the end of their shifts."

item.title: Supreme Court upholds birthright citizenship, blocks Trump order
item.description: President Donald Trump attended oral arguments in the case, underscoring his staunch opposition to granting automatic citizenship to many immigrants' babies.

item.title: Michael Burry says he's shorting Caterpillar for the first time after it nearly doubled in the AI-driven rally of 2026
item.description: "Caterpillar jumped out at me," Burry said. "I have never shorted Caterpillar. It has always done great for me on the long side in the past."

item.title: China-linked actors target more than technology as AI competition with U.S. intensifies
item.description: Cyberattacks from China-based entities are on the rise and not just targeting specific tech, analysts warn, as the U.S. AI race intensifies.

item.title: After a soft Nike quarter, we have a big decision to make on the stock
item.description: The results are unlikely to improve Nike's standing in the portfolio

item.title: What's a good student loan interest rate? These lenders offer some of the lowest APRs
item.description: What counts as a "good" student loan interest rate depends on whether you're borrowing a federal or private loan. Here's what to know.

item.title: Trump says Council of Economic Advisers head Pierre Yared is leaving
item.description: The Council of Economic Advisers has provided the president with information and advice on both domestic and international economic policy since 1946.

item.title: Anthropic launches AI drug discovery program, joining tech giants in betting on healthcare
item.description: Anthropic will start an internal drug discovery program, part of a new push to sell artificial intelligence tools to drugmakers.

item.title: Big egg producers settle price inflation probe with DOJ for 53 million eggs — and $3.3M
item.description: Cal-Maine Foods, Versova and Hickman's Egg Ranch agreed to settle allegations of price manipulation by the Department of Justice and 17 states.

item.title: AWS puts $1 billion into new AI unit to embed engineers with customers, joining growing wave
item.description: AWS FDEs will look to leave behind self-sufficient teams with new AI solutions and capabilities in a matter of weeks, the company said.

item.title: American Airlines brings grab-and-go lounge to New York's JFK
item.description: American Airlines is opening a grab-and-go lounge at New York's JFK

item.title: Private equity in youth sports draws bipartisan scrutiny in Congress
item.description: Democratic and Republican members of Congress expressed alarm at the trend of private equity investment in youth sports.

item.title: FDA lets Philip Morris market Zyn nicotine pouches as less harmful than cigarettes
item.description: The Trump administration has expanded access to nicotine products despite concerns from public health critics about youth use and addiction.

item.title: Why Asia still struggles to create its own mega-IPOs like SpaceX
item.description: Asia has no shortage of entrepreneurs, engineers or giant domestic markets. Yet when it comes to producing the kind of blockbuster listings seen in the U.S., the region continues to lag.
